summary: Phase 5 Assessment page testing complete — all 10 user stories passed. Consumer-first /assess route with always-visible 3D beating heart, accessible QRISK3 questionnaire, AI insights, and save-to-history flow working correctly. Patient context flows and camera scan handoff verified.

flaky_endpoints.issue: Mistral API returns HTTP 429 (rate limit exceeded)
flaky_endpoints.impact: AI insights may fail intermittently due to shared API key quota
flaky_endpoints.note: Not a code bug - API works correctly when quota available. Frontend test confirmed successful narrative generation (674 chars). Consider upgrading Mistral plan or implementing better rate limit handling.

passed_tests: US1: /assess page loads with header Assess button, 3D heart canvas (beating-heart-3d), initial HR=72, no JS errors
US2: HR slider updates from 72 to 120 correctly

US3: QRISK3 questionnaire - age stepper (52), sex (male), ethnicity (indian), smoking (ex), height (178), weight (82), BMI auto-calc (25.9), SBP (142), Chol/HDL (5.0), conditions (type2Dm, treatedHtn) all working
US4: Calculate button shows result card with score (20.4%), band (High), advice text, and 6 recommendations with priority tags
US5: AI insights button generates 674-char Mistral narrative, button text changes to 'Regenerate'
US6: Save button shows success toast 'Assessment saved to history' and redirects to /history
US7: History page displays 16 saved assessments with history-scan-card elements
US8: Onboarding flow - step 1 (welcome) → step 2 (consent checkbox) → step 3 → 'Start my heart-risk check' button routes to /assess
US9: Patient detail page (PT-1ABFBE) → 'Run risk check' button routes to /patients/{id}/assess with patient code chip visible
US10: /assess 'Or run a 30-second camera scan' button routes to /aisteth.html without uncaught JS errors
Backend: GET /api/stats returns totalScans, totalAssessments, totalPatients
Backend: POST /api/save-assessment successfully saves assessment with generated ID
